records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Sacrificial Offerings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3067 - Muhammad al-Halabi asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a group of people—does a cow suffice for them?
Imam (as) replied: "As for the hady (sacrificial offering for Hajj), no. But as for the sacrifice (Udhiyah), yes. And the hady suffices in place of the sacrifice."
records.arabicText: 3067 - وَ سَأَلَ مُحَمَّدٌ اَلْحَلَبِيُّ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : عَنِ اَلنَّفَرِ تُجْزِيهِمُ اَلْبَقَرَةُ فَقَالَ «أَمَّا فِي اَلْهَدْيِ فَلاَ وَ أَمَّا فِي اَلْأَضْحَى فَنَعَمْ وَ يُجْزِي اَلْهَدْيُ عَنِ اَلْأُضْحِيَّةِ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3067 - Muhammad al-Halabi asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a group of people—does a cow suffice for them?
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) replied: "As for the hady (sacrificial offering for Hajj), no. But as for the sacrifice (Udhiyah), yes. And the hady suffices in place of the sacrifice."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Sacrificial Offerings
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3068 - Al-Bazanti narrated from Abdul Karim ibn Amr from Saeed ibn Yasar, who said: I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about someone who buys a sheep but does not announce it (as a sacrifice).
Imam (as) replied: "There is no harm whether he announces it or does not announce it."
records.arabicText: 3068 - وَ رَوَى اَلْبَزَنْطِيُّ عَنْ عَبْدِ اَلْكَرِيمِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ يَسَارٍ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَمَّنِ اِشْتَرَى شَاةً وَ لَمْ يُعَرِّفْ بِهَا فَقَالَ «لاَ بَأْسَ عَرَّفَ بِهَا، أَوْ لَمْ يُعَرِّفْ بِهَا ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3068 - Al-Bazanti narrated from Abdul Karim ibn Amr from Saeed ibn Yasar, who said: I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about someone who buys a sheep but does not announce it (as a sacrifice).
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) replied: "There is no harm whether he announces it or does not announce it."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Sacrificial Animals That Become Injured or Perish Before Reaching Their Designated Place and What is Permitted to Eat From Them
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3069 - Muawiyah ibn Ammar narrated from Abu Abdullah (as) regarding a man who brought a sacrificial camel that gave birth.
Imam (as) said: "He should slaughter it and its offspring. If the hady (sacrificial animal) was guaranteed but perished, he should purchase another in its place and another in place of its offspring."
records.arabicText: 3069 - رَوَى مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي رَجُلٍ سَاقَ بَدَنَةً فَنُتِجَتْ قَالَ «يَنْحَرُهَا وَ يَنْحَرُ وَلَدَهَا وَ إِنْ كَانَ اَلْهَدْيُ مَضْمُوناً فَهَلَكَ اِشْتَرَى مَكَانَهَا وَ مَكَانَ وَلَدِهَا».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3069 - Muawiyah ibn Ammar narrated from Abu Abdullah (as) regarding a man who brought a sacrificial camel that gave birth.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "He should slaughter it and its offspring. If the hady (sacrificial animal) was guaranteed but perished, he should purchase another in its place and another in place of its offspring."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Sacrificial Animals That Become Injured or Perish Before Reaching Their Designated Place and What is Permitted to Eat From Them
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3070 - Mansur ibn Hazim narrated from Abu Abdullah (as) regarding a man whose hady (sacrificial animal) goes missing, and another man finds it and slaughters it.
Imam (as) said: "If he slaughtered it in Mina, it suffices for its owner who lost it. But if he slaughtered it outside of Mina, it does not suffice for its owner."
records.arabicText: 3070 - وَ رَوَى مَنْصُورُ بْنُ حَازِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي اَلرَّجُلِ يَضِلُّ هَدْيُهُ فَيَجِدُهُ رَجُلٌ آخَرُ فَيَنْحَرُهُ فَقَالَ «إِنْ كَانَ نَحَرَهُ بِمِنًى فَقَدْ أَجْزَأَ عَنْ صَاحِبِهِ اَلَّذِي ضَلَّ عَنْهُ وَ إِنْ كَانَ نَحَرَهُ فِي غَيْرِ مِنًى لَمْ يُجْزِ عَنْ صَاحِبِهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3070 - Mansur ibn Hazim narrated from Abu Abdullah (as) regarding a man whose hady (sacrificial animal) goes missing, and another man finds it and slaughters it.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "If he slaughtered it in Mina, it suffices for its owner who lost it. But if he slaughtered it outside of Mina, it does not suffice for its owner."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Sacrificial Animals That Become Injured or Perish Before Reaching Their Designated Place and What is Permitted to Eat From Them
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.3071 - Abdul Rahman ibn al-Hajjaj narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said: "If the hady (sacrificial animal) was announced and then got lost afterward, it suffices."
records.arabicText: 3071 - وَ رَوَى عَبْدُ اَلرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ اَلْحَجَّاجِ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «إِذَا عُرِّفَ بِالْهَدْيِ ثُمَّ ضَلَّ بَعْدَ ذَلِكَ فَقَدْ أَجْزَأَ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3071 - Abdul Rahman ibn al-Hajjaj narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If the hady (sacrificial animal) was announced and then got lost afterward, it suffices."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Sacrificial Animals That Become Injured or Perish Before Reaching Their Designated Place and What is Permitted to Eat From Them
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3072 - It is narrated from Hafs ibn al-Bakhtari, who said: I said to Abu Abdullah (as), "A man brought a hady (sacrificial animal), and it perished in a place where he could not find anyone to give it in charity, nor would anyone know that it is a hady."
Imam (as) replied: "He should slaughter it and place a written note on it so that whoever passes by it knows that it is charity."
records.arabicText: 3072 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ حَفْصِ بْنِ اَلْبَخْتَرِيِّ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ رَجُلٌ سَاقَ اَلْهَدْيَ فَعَطِبَ فِي مَوْضِعٍ لاَ يَقْدِرُ عَلَى مَنْ يَتَصَدَّقُ بِهِ عَلَيْهِ وَ لاَ يَعْلَمُ أَنَّهُ هَدْيٌ فَقَالَ «يَنْحَرُهُ وَ يَكْتُبُ كِتَاباً يَضَعُهُ عَلَيْهِ لِيَعْلَمَ مَنْ مَرَّ بِهِ أَنَّهُ صَدَقَةٌ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.3072 - It is narrated from Hafs ibn al-Bakhtari, who said: I said to Abu Abdullah (as), "A man brought a hady (sacrificial animal), and it perished in a place where he could not find anyone to give it in charity, nor would anyone know that it is a hady."
Imam (as) replied:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "He should slaughter it and place a written note on it so that whoever passes by it knows that it is charity."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Sacrificial Animals That Become Injured or Perish Before Reaching Their Designated Place and What is Permitted to Eat From Them
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3073 - Al-Qasim ibn Muhammad narrated from Ali ibn Abi Hamzah, who said: I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a man who brought a sacrificial camel, and it broke down before reaching its designated place or was overtaken by death or destruction.
Imam (as) said: "He should slaughter it if he is able and smear its shoe, which was used to mark it, with its blood so that whoever passes by it knows it has been slaughtered. They may eat from its meat if they wish. If the hady (sacrificial offering) is guaranteed, then he must replace it by purchasing another in its place if it breaks down or perishes. A guaranteed offering is one that is obligatory upon him due to a vow or other obligations. But if it is not guaranteed, and it is merely something he undertook voluntarily, then he is not obligated to replace it unless he wishes to do so voluntarily."
records.arabicText: 3073 - وَ رَوَى اَلْقَاسِمُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ أَبِي حَمْزَةَ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ سَاقَ بَدَنَةً فَانْكَسَرَتْ قَبْلَ أَنْ تَبْلُغَ مَحِلَّهَا أَوْ عَرَضَ لَهَا مَوْتٌ أَوْ هَلاَكٌ قَالَ «يُذَكِّيهَا إِنْ قَدَرَ عَلَى ذَلِكَ وَ يَلْطَخُ نَعْلَهَا اَلَّتِي قُلِّدَتْ بِهَا حَتَّى يَعْلَمَ مَنْ مَرَّ بِهَا أَنَّهَا قَدْ ذُكِّيَتْ فَيَأْكُلَ مِنْ لَحْمِهَا إِنْ أَرَادَ فَإِنْ كَانَ اَلْهَدْيُ مَضْمُوناً فَإِنَّ عَلَيْهِ أَنْ يُعِيدَهُ يَبْتَاعُ مَكَانَ اَلْهَدْيِ إِذَا اِنْكَسَرَ أَوْ هَلَكَ وَ اَلْمَضْمُونُ اَلْوَاجِبُ عَلَيْهِ فِي نَذْرٍ أَوْ غَيْرِهِ فَإِنْ لَمْ يَكُنْ مَضْمُوناً وَ إِنَّمَا هُوَ شَيْءٌ تَطَوَّعَ بِهِ فَلَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ أَنْ يَبْتَاعَ مَكَانَهُ إِلاَّ أَنْ يَشَاءَ أَنْ يَتَطَوَّعَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3073 - Al-Qasim ibn Muhammad narrated from Ali ibn Abi Hamzah, who said: I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a man who brought a sacrificial camel, and it broke down before reaching its designated place or was overtaken by death or destruction.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "He should slaughter it if he is able and smear its shoe, which was used to mark it, with its blood so that whoever passes by it knows it has been slaughtered. They may eat from its meat if they wish. If the hady (sacrificial offering) is guaranteed, then he must replace it by purchasing another in its place if it breaks down or perishes. A guaranteed offering is one that is obligatory upon him due to a vow or other obligations. But if it is not guaranteed, and it is merely something he undertook voluntarily, then he is not obligated to replace it unless he wishes to do so voluntarily."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Sacrificial Animals That Become Injured or Perish Before Reaching Their Designated Place and What is Permitted to Eat From Them
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3074 - It is narrated from Abdul Rahman ibn al-Hajjaj, who said: I asked Abu Ibrahim (as) about a man who purchased a hady (sacrificial offering) for his Tamattu' Hajj, brought it to his home, tied it up, and then it got loose and perished. Does it suffice for him, or must he replace it?
Imam (as) replied: "It does not suffice for him unless he is unable to replace it."
records.arabicText: 3074 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ عَبْدِ اَلرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ اَلْحَجَّاجِ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُ أَبَا إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ اِشْتَرَى هَدْياً لِمُتْعَتِهِ فَأَتَى بِهِ مَنْزِلَهُ فَرَبَطَهُ ثُمَّ اِنْحَلَّ فَهَلَكَ هَلْ يُجْزِيهِ أَوْ يُعِيدُ قَالَ «لاَ يُجْزِيهِ إِلاَّ أَنْ يَكُونَ لاَ قُوَّةَ بِهِ عَلَيْهِ
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3074 - It is narrated from Abdul Rahman ibn al-Hajjaj, who said: I asked Abu Ibrahim (as) about a man who purchased a hady (sacrificial offering) for his Tamattu' Hajj, brought it to his home, tied it up, and then it got loose and perished. Does it suffice for him, or must he replace it?
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) replied: "It does not suffice for him unless he is unable to replace it."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Sacrificial Animals That Become Injured or Perish Before Reaching Their Designated Place and What is Permitted to Eat From Them
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3075 - Ibn Muskan narrated from Abu Basir, who said: I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a man who purchased a ram, and it perished.
Imam (as) said: "He should purchase another in its place."
I said: "What if he purchases another in its place and then finds the first one?"
Imam (as) replied: "If both are alive, he should slaughter the first one and sell the other, or if he wishes, he may slaughter it as well. If he has already slaughtered the second one, then he should slaughter the first one along with it."
records.arabicText: 3075 - وَ رَوَى اِبْنُ مُسْكَانَ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ اِشْتَرَى كَبْشاً فَهَلَكَ مِنْهُ قَالَ «يَشْتَرِي مَكَانَهُ آخَرَ» قُلْتُ فَإِنِ اِشْتَرَى مَكَانَهُ ثُمَّ وَجَدَ اَلْأَوَّلَ قَالَ «إِنْ كَانَا جَمِيعاً قَائِمَيْنِ فَلْيَذْبَحِ اَلْأَوَّلَ وَ لْيَبِعِ اَلْآخَرَ وَ إِنْ شَاءَ ذَبَحَهُ وَ إِنْ كَانَ قَدْ ذَبَحَ اَلْآخَرَ فَلْيَذْبَحِ اَلْأَوَّلَ مَعَهُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3075 - Ibn Muskan narrated from Abu Basir, who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a man who purchased a ram, and it perished.
Imam (as) said: "He should purchase another in its place."
I said: "What if he purchases another in its place and then finds the first one?"
Imam (as) replied: "If both are alive, he should slaughter the first one and sell the other, or if he wishes, he may slaughter it as well. If he has already slaughtered the second one, then he should slaughter the first one along with it."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Sacrificial Animals That Become Injured or Perish Before Reaching Their Designated Place and What is Permitted to Eat From Them
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.3076 - Muawiyah ibn Ammar narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said: "If a man comes across a lost sacrificial camel, he should slaughter it and announce that it is a sacrificial animal."
records.arabicText: 3076 - وَ رَوَى مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «إِذَا أَصَابَ اَلرَّجُلُ بَدَنَةً ضَالَّةً فَلْيَنْحَرْهَا وَ يُعْلِمُ أَنَّهَا بَدَنَةٌ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3076 - Muawiyah ibn Ammar narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If a man comes across a lost sacrificial camel, he should slaughter it and announce that it is a sacrificial animal."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Sacrificial Animals That Become Injured or Perish Before Reaching Their Designated Place and What is Permitted to Eat From Them
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3077 - Al-Ala narrated from Muhammad ibn Muslim, from one of the Imams (as), who said:
I asked him about a hady (obligatory sacrificial animal) that suffers a fracture or injury—can it be sold, and if sold, what should be done with its price?
Imam (as) replied: "If he sells it, he should give its price in charity and offer another sacrificial animal."
records.arabicText: 3077 - وَ رَوَى اَلْعَلاَءُ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَحَدِهِمَا عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ اَلْهَدْيِ اَلْوَاجِبِ إِنْ أَصَابَهُ كَسْرٌ أَوْ عَطَبٌ أَ يَبِيعُهُ وَ إِنْ بَاعَهُ مَا يَصْنَعُ بِثَمَنِهِ قَالَ «إِنْ بَاعَهُ فَلْيَتَصَدَّقْ بِثَمَنِهِ وَ يُهْدِي هَدْياً آخَرَ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.3077 - Al-Ala narrated from Muhammad ibn Muslim, from one of the Imams (as), who said:
I asked him about a hady (obligatory sacrificial animal) that suffers a fracture or injury—can it be sold, and if sold, what should be done with its price?
Imam (as) replied:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If he sells it, he should give its price in charity and offer another sacrificial animal."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Sacrificial Animals That Become Injured or Perish Before Reaching Their Designated Place and What is Permitted to Eat From Them
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.3078 - In a narration from Hammad, from Hariz, in a Hadith that states at its conclusion: "The guaranteed hady (sacrificial animal) must not be eaten from if it perishes. If one eats from it, they must compensate for it."
records.arabicText: 3078 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ حَمَّادٍ عَنْ حَرِيزٍ فِي حَدِيثٍ يَقُولُ فِي آخِرِهِ : «إِنَّ اَلْهَدْيَ اَلْمَضْمُونَ لاَ يَأْكُلُ مِنْهُ إِذَا عَطِبَ فَإِنْ أَكَلَ مِنْهُ غَرِمَ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3078 - In a narration from Hammad, from Hariz, in a Hadith that states at its conclusion:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The guaranteed hady (sacrificial animal) must not be eaten from if it perishes. If one eats from it, they must compensate for it."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Slaughtering and Sacrificing and What is to Be Said During Slaughter
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.3079 - Muawiyah ibn Ammar narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said: "Nahr (slaughtering a camel) is at the throat hollow, and Dhabh (slaughtering other animals) is at the hollow of the neck."
records.arabicText: 3079 - رَوَى مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «اَلنَّحْرُ فِي اَللَّبَّةِ وَ اَلذَّبْحُ فِي اَلْحَلْقِ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3079 - Muawiyah ibn Ammar narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Nahr (slaughtering a camel) is at the throat hollow, and Dhabh (slaughtering other animals) is at the hollow of the neck."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Slaughtering and Sacrificing and What is to Be Said During Slaughter
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.3080 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said: "Every camel slaughtered in the manner of Dhabh (slaughtering at the neck) is unlawful, and every other animal slaughtered in the manner of Nahr (piercing at the throat hollow) is unlawful."
records.arabicText: 3080 - وَ قَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «كُلُّ مَنْحُورٍ مَذْبُوحٍ حَرَامٌ وَ كُلُّ مَذْبُوحٍ مَنْحُورٍ حَرَامٌ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3080 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Every camel slaughtered in the manner of Dhabh (slaughtering at the neck) is unlawful, and every other animal slaughtered in the manner of Nahr (piercing at the throat hollow) is unlawful."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Slaughtering and Sacrificing and What is to Be Said During Slaughter
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3081 - Al-Halabi narrated from Imam (as) that he said: "A Jew or a Christian should not slaughter your sacrificial animal for you. If it is a woman, she should slaughter for herself, face the Qiblah, and say:
'I have turned my face towards the One who created the heavens and the earth, inclining to the truth, as a submitter [to Allah (swt)]. O Allah (swt), this is from You and for You.'"
records.arabicText: 3081 - وَ رَوَى اَلْحَلَبِيُّ عَنْهُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ : «لاَ يَذْبَحْ لَكَ اَلْيَهُودِيُّ وَ لاَ اَلنَّصْرَانِيُّ أُضْحِيَّتَكَ وَ إِنْ كَانَتِ اِمْرَأَةٌ فَلْتَذْبَحْ لِنَفْسِهَا وَ تَسْتَقْبِلُ اَلْقِبْلَةَ وَ تَقُولُ: «وَجَّهْتُ وَجْهِيَ لِلَّذِي فَطَرَ اَلسَّمٰاوٰاتِ وَ اَلْأَرْضَ حَنِيفاً» مُسْلِماً اَللَّهُمَّ مِنْكَ وَ لَكَ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3081 - Al-Halabi narrated from Imam (as) that he said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"A Jew or a Christian should not slaughter your sacrificial animal for you. If it is a woman, she should slaughter for herself, face the Qiblah, and say:
'I have turned my face towards the One who created the heavens and the earth, inclining to the truth, as a submitter [to Allah (swt)]. O Allah (swt), this is from You and for You.'"

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Slaughtering and Sacrificing and What is to Be Said During Slaughter
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3082 - Abdullah ibn Sinan narrated from Imam Abu Abdullah (as) regarding the words of Allah (swt), the Exalted: "So mention the name of Allah (swt) upon them while they are lined up [for sacrifice]" (Surah Al-Hajj 22:36).
Imam (as) said: "This refers to when they are positioned for sacrifice, and their forelegs are tied from the hooves to the knees, and their sides fall to the ground after they are slaughtered."
records.arabicText:
3082 - وَ رَوَى عَبْدُ اَللَّهِ بْنُ سِنَانٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي قَوْلِ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ
«۝ فَاذْكُرُوا اِسْمَ اَللّٰهِ عَلَيْهٰا صَوٰافَّ ۝» قَالَ «ذَلِكَ حِينَ تَصُفُّ لِلنَّحْرِ وَ تَرْبِطُ يَدَيْهَا مَا بَيْنَ اَلْخُفِّ إِلَى اَلرُّكْبَةِ وَ وُجُوبُ جُنُوبِهَا إِذَا وَقَعَتْ إِلَى اَلْأَرْضِ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.3082 - Abdullah ibn Sinan narrated from Imam Abu Abdullah (as) regarding the words of Allah (swt), the Exalted: "So mention the name of Allah (swt) upon them while they are lined up [for sacrifice]" (Surah Al-Hajj 22:36).
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "This refers to when they are positioned for sacrifice, and their forelegs are tied from the hooves to the knees, and their sides fall to the ground after they are slaughtered."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Slaughtering and Sacrificing and What is to Be Said During Slaughter
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3083 - Abu al-Sabbah al-Kinani asked Imam (as): "How is the camel (badanah) to be slaughtered?"
Imam (as) replied: "It is slaughtered while standing, from its right side."
records.arabicText: 3083 - وَ سَأَلَهُ أَبُو اَلصَّبَّاحِ اَلْكِنَانِيُّ : كَيْفَ تُنْحَرُ اَلْبَدَنَةُ قَالَ «تُنْحَرُ وَ هِيَ قَائِمَةٌ مِنْ قِبَلِ اَلْيَمِينِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.3083 - Abu al-Sabbah al-Kinani asked Imam (as): "How is the camel (badanah) to be slaughtered?"
Imam (as) replied:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "It is slaughtered while standing, from its right side."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Slaughtering and Sacrificing and What is to Be Said During Slaughter
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3084 - Muawiyah ibn Ammar narrated from Imam (as) that he said: "When you purchase your hady (sacrificial animal), face it towards the Qiblah and slaughter or sacrifice it, and say:
'I have turned my face towards the One who created the heavens and the earth, inclining to the truth, as a submitter [to Allah (swt)], and I am not among the polytheists. Indeed, my prayer, my sacrifice, my life, and my death are for Allah (swt), the Lord (azj) of the worlds. He has no partner, and with this, I have been commanded, and I am among the submitters. O Allah (swt), this is from You and for You. In name of Allah (swt), Allah (swt) is the Greatest. O Allah (swt), accept it from me.'
Then pass the knife over it, but do not sever its spinal cord until it dies."
records.arabicText: 3084 - وَ رَوَى مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ عَنْهُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ : «إِذَا اِشْتَرَيْتَ هَدْيَكَ فَاسْتَقْبِلْ بِهِ اَلْقِبْلَةَ وَ اِنْحَرْهُ أَوِ اِذْبَحْهُ وَ قُلْ «وَجَّهْتُ وَجْهِيَ لِلَّذِي فَطَرَ اَلسَّمٰاوٰاتِ وَ اَلْأَرْضَ حَنِيفاً» مُسْلِماً «وَ مٰا أَنَا مِنَ اَلْمُشْرِكِينَ » «إِنَّ صَلاٰتِي وَ نُسُكِي وَ مَحْيٰايَ وَ مَمٰاتِي لِلّٰهِ رَبِّ اَلْعٰالَمِينَ `لاٰ شَرِيكَ لَهُ وَ بِذٰلِكَ أُمِرْتُ» وَ أَنَا مِنَ اَلْمُسْلِمِينَ اَللَّهُمَّ مِنْكَ وَ لَكَ بِسْمِ اَللَّهِ وَ اَللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ اَللَّهُمَّ تَقَبَّلْ مِنِّي ثُمَّ أَمِرَّ اَلسِّكِّينَ وَ لاَ تَنْخَعْهَا حَتَّى تَمُوتَ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3084 - Muawiyah ibn Ammar narrated from Imam (as) that he said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"When you purchase your hady (sacrificial animal), face it towards the Qiblah and slaughter or sacrifice it, and say:
'I have turned my face towards the One who created the heavens and the earth, inclining to the truth, as a submitter [to Allah (swt)], and I am not among the polytheists. Indeed, my prayer, my sacrifice, my life, and my death are for Allah (swt), the Lord (azj) of the worlds. He has no partner, and with this, I have been commanded, and I am among the submitters. O Allah (swt), this is from You and for You. In name of Allah (swt), Allah (swt) is the Greatest. O Allah (swt), accept it from me.'
Then pass the knife over it, but do not sever its spinal cord until it dies."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Offspring, Milking, and Riding of Sacrificial Animals
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3085 - Hammad narrated from Hariz that Abu Abdullah (as) said:
"When Imam Ali (as) brought the sacrificial camels, he would let the pedestrians ride on a camel. If a man's riding animal was lost and he had a sacrificial camel, he could ride it, provided he did not harm it or overburden it."
records.arabicText: 3085 - رَوَى حَمَّادٌ عَنْ حَرِيزٍ أَنَّ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «كَانَ عَلِيٌّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ إِذَا سَاقَ اَلْبَدَنَةَ وَ مَرَّ عَلَى اَلْمُشَاةِ حَمَلَهُمْ عَلَى بَدَنَةٍ وَ إِنْ ضَلَّتْ رَاحِلَةُ رَجُلٍ وَ مَعَهُ بَدَنَةٌ رَكِبَهَا غَيْرَ مُضِرٍّ وَ لاَ مُثْقِلٍ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3085 - Hammad narrated from Hariz that Abu Abdullah (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "When Imam Ali (as) brought the sacrificial camels, he would let the pedestrians ride on a camel. If a man's riding animal was lost and he had a sacrificial camel, he could ride it, provided he did not harm it or overburden it."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Offspring, Milking, and Riding of Sacrificial Animals
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3086 - Ya'qub ibn Shu'ayb asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a man—can he ride his hady (sacrificial animal) if he needs to?
Imam (as) replied: "The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) said: 'He may ride it as long as he does not overburden or exhaust it.'"
records.arabicText: 3086 - وَ سَأَلَ يَعْقُوبُ بْنُ شُعَيْبٍ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ أَ يَرْكَبُ هَدْيَهُ إِنِ اِحْتَاجَ إِلَيْهِ فَقَالَ «قَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ «يَرْكَبُهَا غَيْرَ مُجْهِدٍ وَ لاَ مُتْعِبٍ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.3086 - Ya'qub ibn Shu'ayb asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a man—can he ride his hady (sacrificial animal) if he needs to?
Imam (as) replied:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) said: 'He may ride it as long as he does not overburden or exhaust it.'"

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Offspring, Milking, and Riding of Sacrificial Animals
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3087 - Mansur ibn Hazim narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"Imam Ali (as) used to milk the sacrificial camel and allow others to ride it, provided it was not harmed."
records.arabicText: 3087 - وَ رَوَى مَنْصُورُ بْنُ حَازِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «كَانَ عَلِيٌّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يَحْلُبُ اَلْبَدَنَةَ وَ يَحْمِلُ عَلَيْهَا غَيْرَ مُضِرٍّ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3087 - Mansur ibn Hazim narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Imam Ali (as) used to milk the sacrificial camel and allow others to ride it, provided it was not harmed."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Offspring, Milking, and Riding of Sacrificial Animals
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3088 - Abu Basir narrated from Imam (as) regarding the words of Allah (swt), the Exalted: "For you, there are benefits in them until a specified term" (Surah Al-Hajj 22:33).
Imam (as) said: "If one needs to use its back, he may ride it without being harsh on it. And if it has milk, he may milk it in a way that does not exhaust it."
records.arabicText: 3088 - وَ رَوَى أَبُو بَصِيرٍ عَنْهُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي قَوْلِ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ «لَكُمْ فِيهٰا مَنٰافِعُ إِلىٰ أَجَلٍ مُسَمًّى » قَالَ «إِنِ اِحْتَاجَ إِلَى ظَهْرِهَا رَكِبَهَا مِنْ غَيْرِ أَنْ يَعْنُفَ عَلَيْهَا وَ إِنْ كَانَ لَهَا لَبَنٌ حَلَبَهَا حِلاَباً لاَ يَنْهَكُهَا ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3088 - Abu Basir narrated from Imam (as) regarding the words of Allah (swt), the Exalted: "For you, there are benefits in them until a specified term" (Surah Al-Hajj 22:33).
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "If one needs to use its back, he may ride it without being harsh on it. And if it has milk, he may milk it in a way that does not exhaust it."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Sacrificial Animal Reaching Its Designated Place
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3089 - Ali ibn Abi Hamzah narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"If a man purchases his hady (sacrificial animal) and confines it in his house, it has reached its designated place. If he wishes, he may shave his head."
records.arabicText: 3089 - روى رَوَى عَلِيُّ بْنُ أَبِي حَمْزَةَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «إِذَا اِشْتَرَى اَلرَّجُلُ هَدْيَهُ وَ قَمَطَهُ فِي بَيْتِهِ فَقَدْ بَلَغَ مَحِلَّهُ فَإِنْ شَاءَ فَلْيَحْلِقْ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3089 - Ali ibn Abi Hamzah narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If a man purchases his hady (sacrificial animal) and confines it in his house, it has reached its designated place. If he wishes, he may shave his head."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on a Person Who Assigns Another to Slaughter on Their Behalf and Throws Their Hair in Mecca
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3090 - Ibn Muskan narrated from Abu Basir, who said: I said to Abu Abdullah (as): "A man instructs someone to slaughter on his behalf while he discards his hair in Mecca."
Imam (as) replied: "He is not permitted to discard his hair except in Mina."
records.arabicText: 3090 - رَوَى اِبْنُ مُسْكَانَ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ اَلرَّجُلُ يُوصِي مَنْ يَذْبَحُ عَنْهُ وَ يُلْقِي هُوَ شَعْرَهُ بِمَكَّةَ فَقَالَ «لَيْسَ لَهُ أَنْ يُلْقِيَ شَعْرَهُ إِلاَّ بِمِنًى ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3090 - Ibn Muskan narrated from Abu Basir, who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I said to Abu Abdullah (as): "A man instructs someone to slaughter on his behalf while he discards his hair in Mecca."
Imam (as) replied: "He is not permitted to discard his hair except in Mina."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Advancing or Delaying the Rituals
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3091 - Ibn Abi Umair narrated from Jamil ibn Darraj, from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
I asked him about a man who visits the Kaaba before shaving his head.
Imam (as) said: "It is not appropriate unless he has forgotten."
Then Imam (as) said: "On the Day of Sacrifice, some people came to the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) and said, 'O Messenger of Allah (swt), I shaved my head before slaughtering,' and some said, 'I shaved my head before throwing the pebbles.' They did not leave anything they were supposed to do earlier except that they delayed it, and nothing they were supposed to delay except that they did it earlier. The Prophet said, 'There is no harm.'"
records.arabicText: 3091 - رَوَى اِبْنُ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنْ جَمِيلِ بْنِ دَرَّاجٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يَزُورُ اَلْبَيْتَ قَبْلَ أَنْ يَحْلِقَ قَالَ «لاَ يَنْبَغِي إِلاَّ أَنْ يَكُونَ نَاسِياً » ثُمَّ قَالَ «إِنَّ رَسُولَ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ أَتَاهُ أُنَاسٌ، يَوْمَ اَلنَّحْرِ فَقَالَ بَعْضُهُمْ يَا رَسُولَ اَللَّهِ حَلَقْتُ قَبْلَ أَنْ أَذْبَحَ وَ قَالَ بَعْضُهُمْ حَلَقْتُ قَبْلَ أَنْ أَرْمِيَ فَلَمْ يَتْرُكُوا شَيْئاً كَانَ يَنْبَغِي لَهُمْ أَنْ يُقَدِّمُوهُ إِلاَّ أَخَّرُوهُ وَ لاَ شَيْئاً كَانَ يَنْبَغِي لَهُمْ أَنْ يُؤَخِّرُوهُ إِلاَّ قَدَّمُوهُ فَقَالَ «لاَ حَرَجَ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.3091 - Ibn Abi Umair narrated from Jamil ibn Darraj, from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
I asked him about a man who visits the Kaaba before shaving his head.
records.thaqalaynMatn:
Imam (as) said: "It is not appropriate unless he has forgotten."
Then Imam (as) said: "On the Day of Sacrifice, some people came to the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) and said, 'O Messenger of Allah (swt), I shaved my head before slaughtering,' and some said, 'I shaved my head before throwing the pebbles.' They did not leave anything they were supposed to do earlier except that they delayed it, and nothing they were supposed to delay except that they did it earlier. The Prophet said, 'There is no harm.'"
